summary: Stunning soundboard capture of the April 18‑1982 Hartford show – crystal‑clear mix paired with a powerhouse set highlighted by Althea, Space, Let It Grow and a blistering Big Railroad Blues.
review: The recording is an excellent soundboard tape with a balanced, crisp mix that lets every instrument shine.  The performance is frequently described as a “powerhouse” – the first set rolls tight while the second set explodes on Space, Althea, Let It Grow and a monster Big Railroad Blues, with Jerry’s solos and Brent’s Hammond work getting special praise.  Overall fans rate the show overwhelmingly positive despite a few minor comparative comments about an audience‑only version.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 fan reviews, most 4–5 stars, repeatedly call the show a classic and the board recording “excellent”.  Minor criticisms are limited to comparative comments with an audience (AUD) version, so the combined weighted score is very high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as a well‑balanced mix, crisp and clear; reviewers note “amazing board”, “great recording” and “pristine snapshot”.  One comment mentions the audience (AUD) version sounding “under water”, reinforcing that the soundboard is superior.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans praise a tight, rocking first set and an unstoppable second set that builds into epic jams (Space, Samson, Playing in the Band > Eyes).  The show’s pacing is described as “powerhouse” with every transition feeling natural and exciting.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly called "amazing" – ripping solos on Let It Grow, Ship of Fools and throughout the second set; his voice noted as excellent.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for a “space quake” – hysterical in Space, powerful bass solo that shook the venue, and overall energetic presence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for strong vocal delivery on Althea and rhythmic slide work on Samson; rhythm guitar described as “studied by all rhythm guitarists”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond organ work singled out on Big Railroad Blues and Let It Grow, with many reviewers calling his playing "slashing" and "great".
band_member_comments.Drums: The Rhythm Devils receive enthusiastic mentions – drums/jam sections described as “awesome”, “mind‑bender” and a highlight of the second set.
